conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to gather some information about your circumstances. Let me start by asking:

Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm asking about my son Alex. He's 17 and lives with me here in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask a few questions.

First, could you tell me: Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. He's studying for his A-levels at the local sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you! A-levels at sixth form college would typically count as full-time non-advanced education.

Now, could you tell me: Are you responsible for Alex? By this I mean, does he live with you and do you have primary responsibility for his care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. He lives with me and I'm responsible for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

Are you or your partner currently in the UK, or are you subject to immigration control that would prevent you from claiming public funds?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I live in the UK, yes. There's no immigration issues - I'm able to claim public funds.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for that information. Just one final question:

Is there anyone else who might also be claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or is anyone else responsible for him (for example, does he live with anyone else or spend equal time between two households)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, there's nobody else claiming for him. He lives with me full-time, and nobody else is getting Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me and the official Child Benefit rules:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is 17 and studying A-levels at sixth form college, which counts as approved full-time non-advanced education
- He lives with you full-time and you're responsible for his care
- You live in the UK and can claim public funds
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You can continue to receive Child Benefit for Alex until he turns 20, as long as he remains in approved education or training.

**Please note:** If either you or your partner has an individual income over £60,000, you may be subject to the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. You would still receive the benefit, but may need to pay some or all of it back through your tax return.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit through the government website or by post. If you have any other questions, please feel free to ask!

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. He is 17 years old and in full-time non-advanced education (studying A-levels at sixth form college), which means he qualifies as a young person under 20 in approved education. You are responsible for Alex as he lives with you full-time and you provide his care. You live in the UK and have no immigration restrictions preventing you from claiming public funds. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to the gov.uk eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16, and this extends to children under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. All conditions are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 17-year-old son Alex. Alex lives with the claimant full-time, is in approved full-time non-advanced education (A-levels), and the claimant is responsible for his care. The claimant lives in the UK with no immigration restrictions, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are satisfied.
